title: practical english usage - Part 136
content:
However, if it is used with a singular noun, any following verb is singular.

The majority of criminals are non-violent.

verbs.

The majority of (= 'most' or 'most of) is mostly used with plural nouns and 5 the    majority         of a great deal, but the usage is quite common in standard English speech.

Some people think it is a mistake to use a plural noun after a large amount or A large amount of problems... or A  great deal of problems...) A large number of problems still have to be solved. (More common than A large number of is used before plurals, and a following verb is plural.

Determiners:       Quantifiers              13 grammar •  Section 14   Pronouns with their other uses.

These pronoun-like uses of determiners are covered in ►    Sections 12-13,  along Not all bears can climb trees, but most can. (= 'most bears’ ) ’ Which scarf would you like?' ’ I’ll take both.’ (= 'both scarves’ ) Look at this. (= ’ this thing that you can see’ ) nouns, like pronouns, when the determiner alone makes the meaning clear.

Some determiners (e.g. this, both, most) can be used alone without following whatever, whichever, ►    252.

For the interrogative pronouns who, what, which, ►    625-627. For whoever, Most pronouns are covered in this Section. For relative pronouns, ►    Section 21.

one - meaning for example 'people in general’ somebody - referring to an unknown or undefined person your - meaning ’ belonging to or associated with the hearer(s)’ it - referring to a thing that has already been mentioned I - meaning ’ the speaker’ unnecessary or impossible to use a more precise expression.
